condition:
Viewed from a slight isometric angle in the rendered image, the assembly presents a vertically oriented grapple with a tapered central hub and four evenly spaced curved jaw arms fanning outward and downward; short cylindrical barrels and slender rods bridge between the hub and the mid-sections of each jaw. The single grounded central body (ae62e424, 1 instance) is a bell-shaped end fitting with a lower cylinder of radius 13.5 mm, a conical transition, an upper cylinder of radius 9.39 mm, and an eye lug bearing a 5 mm through-bore at the crown. Wrapping around the lower cylindrical section are eight thin mounting lugs (ae615d7e, 8 instances, aspect ratio 5.77) each carrying a 2.8 mm through-hole for pin retention; four D-shaped lug tabs (ae59bc4c, 4 instances) and four additional mounting lugs (ae6184a4, 4 instances) similarly interface with the upper cylinder, carrying 1.28 mm and 2.48 mm through-holes respectively. The jaw arms come in two mirrored variants — gripper tines (ae4cc3e8, 2 instances) and gripper jaws (ae7c8674, 2 instances) — each roughly 73.6 mm long with B-spline-curved profiles, longitudinal stiffening ribs, and two orthogonally oriented 3.6 mm pin holes at the pivot and mid-body positions. Four hollow cylindrical barrels (ae59e39a, 4 instances, ~28 mm long, 0.7 mm wall, domed end) span between the hub and jaw pivot zones; four follower rods (ae61f99c, 4 instances) with mushroom-head caps and transverse 1.2 mm bored bosses provide the linkage connection. Fasteners comprise four stepped pins (ae594702, shank dia 2.7 mm, head dia 3.6 mm), four flanged pins (ae596e1c, shaft dia 1.2 mm), four spool pins (ae610f34, shaft dia 1.2 mm), and four plain cylindrical pins (ae61d29a, dia ~1.08 mm). All 26 hole-link inferences show near-zero perpendicular distance (≤ 0.016 mm), confirming tight coaxial alignment. Layout analysis identifies 4-fold rotational symmetry about the Y-axis for eight different part classes and 4 mirror pairs corresponding to the two jaw variants and their associated curved support arms (ae44d4ac, ae6666ac, 2 instances each).

Parts:
- curved_support_arm (×2): A thin, elongated strip roughly 73.6 mm long with an aspect ratio of about 8.65, sweeping through a gentle S- or C-curve defined by freeform (B-spline) surfaces on its broad faces and flat planar side walls. A small tab protrudes near one end, likely serving as a locating or retention interface. With no holes, two instances in the assembly, and edge fillets at R ≈ 1.0–2.1 mm along the edges, this part appears to function as a curved structural support arm or rib, possibly arranged as a symmetric pair.
- gripper_tine (×2): A curved, tapered arm approximately 73.6 mm long with an aspect ratio of 4.32, featuring longitudinal ribs and freeform B-spline surfaces along its length. An eye lug at the top carries a 3.6 mm through-hole oriented along one axis, while a mid-body tab carries a second 3.6 mm through-hole oriented orthogonally, both likely accepting pins for pivoting or linkage connections. The part exists in two instances and tapers to a narrow pointed tip. Given the paired occurrence, dual pivot-capable holes, ribbed stiffening, and claw-like profile, this part appears to function as a tine or jaw in a gripping or clamping mechanism.
- stepped_pin (×4): A small solid stepped cylindrical pin roughly 8.4 mm long and 3.6 mm in maximum diameter (aspect ratio ≈ 2.33). It features three coaxial cylindrical sections—a long main shank (R 1.35 mm, ≈ 7.2 mm long), a short intermediate shoulder (R 1.55 mm, ≈ 0.4 mm), and a wider head (R 1.8 mm, ≈ 0.4 mm)—connected by four toroidal fillet blends, with flat end and annular step faces. With no holes and four instances in the assembly, it likely serves as a shoulder pin or dowel that locates or retains mating components via its stepped diameter transitions.
- flanged_pin (×4): An axially symmetric, spool-shaped solid roughly 8.4 mm long with an aspect ratio of about 2.33, featuring a central shaft of radius 0.6 mm flanked by two disc flanges of different sizes — a large head at radius 1.8 mm and a smaller shoulder at radius 0.95 mm, both approximately 0.41 mm thick. Four toroidal blend surfaces create smooth fillet transitions at each flange junction. With no through-holes, four instances in the assembly, and flat end faces normal to the part axis, this part likely serves as a headed pin or tappet-like locating element whose large flat face could provide a mating or bearing surface while the shaft seats into a receiving bore.
- lug_tab (×4): A thin, D-shaped plate (bbox approximately 1.47 × 5.70 × 9.01 mm, aspect ratio 6.13) with one straight end and one semicircular end (outer radius 9.39 mm). A small through-hole of diameter approximately 1.28 mm passes through the 1.47 mm thickness near the rounded end, surrounded by a concentric cylindrical feature of radius 2.85 mm. Two large opposing flat faces provide potential mating surfaces, while the through-hole could receive a pin or small fastener. Instanced four times in the assembly, this part likely serves as a mounting lug or pivot tab.
- cylindrical_pin_with_retaining_features (×4): An elongated hollow cylindrical body approximately 24 mm long and 6.76 mm in outer diameter (aspect ratio 4.19), capped at one end by a smooth dome formed from B-spline surfaces and open or stepped at the other end. Two small radial bosses protrude from the cylinder wall, each pierced by a through-hole of diameter ~0.864 mm that could accept a retaining pin or clip. Longitudinal flat cuts (indicated by opposing plane normals perpendicular to the main axis) appear to provide anti-rotation capability. With four instances in the assembly and its combination of dome cap, hollow bore, and cross-pin holes, this part likely serves as a cylindrical piston or retaining pin element.
- spool_pin (×4): A small spool-shaped solid of revolution (~5.46 mm long, aspect ratio ~1.52) featuring a large circular flange (R 1.8 mm), a connecting cylindrical shaft (R 0.6 mm, ~4.28 mm long), and a smaller flange (R 0.95 mm), with filleted transitions throughout (4 torus faces). No holes are present; the part appears in four instances in the assembly. It likely serves as a stepped pin or post, where the larger flange could act as a stop surface and the smaller flange could provide retention against mating components.
- mounting_lug (×8): A thin, plate-like body (aspect ratio ~5.8, extents 1.47 × 7.48 × 8.48) with a roughly square profile and one large quarter-circle rounded corner (R = 13.5), giving it a D-shaped silhouette. A single through-hole of diameter 2.8 passes through the 1.47-thick dimension, appearing centrally positioned within the face. Two fillet blends (R ≈ 2.45) smooth the transitions between the rounded corner and the straight edges. Used in 8 instances in the assembly, this part likely serves as a mounting lug or clevis ear, where the through-hole could receive a pin or fastener and the flat faces could mate against adjacent structure.
- mounting_lug (×4): A thin flat plate (≈9.0 × 5.7 × 1.5 mm, aspect ratio 6.1) with a rectangular attachment end and a semicircular eye end featuring a through-hole of diameter ≈2.48 mm through the 1.47 mm thickness. The eye's outer contour has a radius of ≈2.85 mm, and a large-radius (≈9.39 mm) curved transition blends the straight and rounded sections. Present in four grounded instances with no recorded contact pairs, this part appears to serve as a mounting lug or hinge ear whose through-hole could accept a pin or small fastener while the flat rectangular end likely attaches rigidly to a supporting structure.
- cylindrical_pin (×4): A solid, featureless cylinder with a single outer radius of 0.5382 and an axial extent of approximately 1.084, yielding a nearly equal diameter-to-height proportion (aspect ratio 1.378). It has only three faces — one cylindrical surface and two flat circular end faces — with no holes or internal features, and a volume consistent with a fully solid body. Four instances appear in the assembly, at least one grounded, suggesting this part likely functions as a locating pin, dowel, or short cylindrical spacer that could seat into mating bores.
- follower_rod (×4): A slender elongated body (aspect ratio ~4.4, bbox ~5 × 15.5 × 22.3 mm) consisting of a thin mushroom-head disc at one end (radius ~2.53 mm), a long cylindrical stem (radius ~0.54 mm, ~16.6 mm long), and a transverse cylindrical boss at the opposite end containing a through-hole of diameter 1.20 mm. Two toroidal fillets blend the stem into the head and boss. Four instances exist in the assembly. The through-hole likely permits a pivot pin, and the disc head could serve as a bearing or contact surface, suggesting this part functions as a follower rod or valve-like linkage element.
- end_fitting_with_eye: A bell-shaped solid body approximately 27 × 27 × 43.5 mm (aspect ratio ~1.61) featuring a wide cylindrical base (R 13.5 mm), a conical taper to a narrower upper cylinder (R 9.39 mm), and a flat protruding lug at the top with a rounded profile (R ~5.07 mm) containing a 5 mm-diameter through-bore (3 mm deep). Eight planar faces with normals in all six cardinal directions reflect the rectangular lug atop the axially-symmetric body. The through-hole in the lug likely permits a pin or hook connection, suggesting this part could function as a terminal end-fitting or anchor eye.
- curved_arm (×2): A long, slender, gently curved solid body approximately 73.6 mm in length with an aspect ratio of 8.648, featuring broad B-spline-surfaced flanks, flat planar side walls, and a beveled lower end. At the upper tip, a small cylindrical protrusion of R = 1.0 mm extends about 11 mm, terminating in a rounded dome and blended into the main body with fillet transitions. No through-holes or recorded contact pairs are present; two instances exist in the assembly, suggesting a mirrored pair. The part likely serves as a curved arm or tine, with its pin-like tip possibly permitting insertion into a mating bore.
- gripper_jaw (×2): An elongated, gently curved arm approximately 73.6 mm long with an aspect ratio of 4.32, tapering from a ribbed mid-section to a narrow tip. It features two 3.6 mm-diameter through-holes: one at the top lug oriented along the Z-axis and one in the mid-body oriented along the X-axis, both likely serving as pin or pivot connections. B-spline surfaces form the smoothly sculpted outer profile, while longitudinal ribs and planar side walls stiffen the body. Two instances exist in the assembly, suggesting a symmetric pair; the overall tapered claw-like form and dual pin-holes indicate the part could function as a jaw or finger of a gripper or clamp, pivoting about the upper hole.

Annotation Caveats:
The contact_edge_counts dictionary is empty, so all inter-part connectivity is inferred solely from the 26 hole-link edges; no conventional planar or cylindrical mating contacts were recorded. Several large-diameter hole-link clusters (27 mm and 18.78 mm) actually represent coaxial cylindrical wrap-fits between lug outer profiles and the central body's cylindrical sections, not true bores — the hole detector misinterprets these curved perimeter surfaces as through-holes. The 5 mm bore in the eye lug of the end fitting (ae62e424) and the ~0.86 mm radial holes on the cylindrical barrels (ae59e39a) have no matching fastener in the hole_edges_summary, meaning either no fastener body is modeled or the match fell outside detection thresholds. The four plain cylindrical pins (ae61d29a) likewise appear in no hole-link cluster. All 49 instances are marked grounded, preventing any kinematic inference from the assembly tree; the flat tree depth of 1 indicates no sub-assembly grouping.
